description: NPN general-purpose BJT transistor — small signal, 45V, 100mA, low noise. European complement to 2N2222 (lower current rating).

pins.name: C
pins.description: Collector. Load connects between Vcc and collector.
pins.requirements: BC547 TO-92 pinout (flat face): C, B, E left-to-right — OPPOSITE to 2N2222. Very common source of errors.

pins.name: B
pins.description: Base. Requires base resistor from driver.
pins.requirements: Base resistor required. Rb = (Vsource - 0.7) / Ib. For saturation: Ib ≥ Ic / (hFE/10).

pins.name: E
pins.description: Emitter. Connect to GND in typical NPN low-side switch.
pins.requirements: Connect to GND or emitter resistor to GND.

required_passives.value: Base resistor (10kΩ–100kΩ typical for small signals)
required_passives.placement: In series at base from driver
required_passives.reason: Limits base current — required to prevent damage to driver

application_circuits.name: NPN low-side switch
application_circuits.description: Load from Vcc to Collector. Emitter to GND. Base resistor from GPIO. GPIO HIGH turns transistor ON.

common_mistakes: BC547 TO-92 pinout is C, B, E — opposite to 2N2222 (E, B, C). Installing one where the other is expected = wrong orientation = circuit failure
Maximum collector current is 100mA — do not use for higher current loads
No base resistor = damage to driving source
hFE varies 110–800 across BC547A/B/C grades — always design for minimum hFE
notes: BC547 variants: A (hFE 110–220), B (hFE 200–450), C (hFE 420–800). Higher gain variants are better for high-impedance base drives. Pinout flat-face left-to-right: C-B-E. Compare with 2N2222 TO-92 flat-face: E-B-C. Common mistake in breadboard substitution.
